fixture.description: IDs are built from currentTimeMillis() plus a Math.random() suffix; two IDs generated in the same millisecond can collide, producing duplicate primary keys.

grader.rubric: PASS if the report flags that nextId() can produce colliding identifiers when called concurrently / multiple times in the same millisecond (timestamp + a bounded Math.random() suffix is not collision-free) -> duplicate primary-key / lost rows, and recommends a guaranteed-unique scheme (AtomicLong sequence, UUID, or DB sequence). Severity IMPORTANT or CRITICAL. FAIL if the collision/uniqueness risk is not surfaced. Do NOT require the fix to be about cryptographic randomness; uniqueness, not unpredictability, is the issue.

expected_behavior: Bugs agent flags the collision-prone ID generation
expected_not_behavior: Does NOT frame it only as a weak-RNG security issue
